acceptance_criterion: Endpoint validates payload against intake schema v2 required_fields
notes: Server reject 400 on missing required field

acceptance_criterion: Endpoint silently rejects payloads containing any forbidden_field name
notes: Never log forbidden field value; audit_event records decision=rejected_forbidden_field

acceptance_criterion: Server resolves runtime_set by joining listing_id against active and staged runtime maps
Queue item file written atomically (temp-file-then-rename)
Queue item file path is under docs/platform-v2/local/usce-corrections/inbox/YYYY/MM/
Server-side redaction pass replaces SSN-shaped / credit-card-shaped / passport-shaped strings in user_message before write
IP stored only as sha256-truncated hash with 24h cache lifetime
Honeypot field non-empty silently rejects with 200 + no queue write
Audit event intake_received always written (success or rejection)
/contact reads searchParams.ref and searchParams.listing_id
/contact visibly echoes the institution being reported when listing_id resolves
notes: User sees what they're reporting against

acceptance_criterion: /contact form submission includes listing_id + ref + runtime_set + source_context in payload
/contact does not include any forbidden_field input or upload widget
/contact does not contain banned phrases (apply through USCEHub / hospital-approved / IMG-friendly / verified by hospital)
Send Message button has a real submit handler that invokes the intake endpoint server action
Invalid listing_id (does not match active or staged runtime) is rejected client-side AND server-side
/contact does not import staged runtime file directly; uses the lookup helper
Queue item validator hard-fails on missing required fields
Queue item validator hard-fails on forbidden fields
Queue item validator hard-fails on invalid status transitions
Audit log validator hard-fails on duplicate audit_event_id
Audit log validator hard-fails on state regression without reopened_with_audit event
CLI defaults to --dry-run and requires explicit --commit to mutate
CLI refuses to mutate any active runtime file
CLI enforces dual-signoff rules per reviewer auth model
CLI appends an audit event for every state transition
CLI prevents same actor_id from approving their own decision
Admin route is gated behind real auth (not just env flag)
Admin route does not appear in sitemap.xml or any nav
Admin route renders only redacted user_message by default; full message requires elevated role
No production deploy until all sprints land + cumulative blast-radius audit + explicit user instruction
No active runtime card content modified by any sprint
